sections.title: Equality before law
sections.text: The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

sections.title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech, etc.
sections.text: (1) All citizens shall have the right— (a) to freedom of speech and expression; (b) to assemble peaceably and without arms; (c) to form associations or unions; (d) to move freely throughout the territory of India; (e) to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India; (g) to practise any profession, or to carry on any occupation, trade or business.

sections.title: Protection of life and personal liberty
sections.text: No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.

sections.title: Right to education
sections.text: The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years in such manner as the State may, by law, determine.

sections.title: Remedies for enforcement of rights conferred by this Part
sections.text: (1) The right to move the Supreme Court by appropriate proceedings for the enforcement of the rights conferred by this Part is guaranteed. (2) The Supreme Court shall have power to issue directions or orders or writs, including writs in the nature of habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari, whichever may be appropriate, for the enforcement of any of the rights conferred by this Part.

sections.title: Power of High Courts to issue certain writs
sections.text: (1) Notwithstanding anything in article 32, every High Court shall have power, throughout the territories in relation to which it exercises jurisdiction, to issue to any person or authority, including in appropriate cases, any Government, within those territories directions, orders or writs, including writs in the nature of habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari, or any of them, for the enforcement of any of the rights conferred by Part III and for any other purpose.

sections.title: Right to property
sections.text: No person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law.

sections.title: Power of Parliament to amend the Constitution
sections.text: (1) Notwithstanding anything in this Constitution, Parliament may in exercise of its constituent power amend by way of addition, variation or repeal any provision of this Constitution in accordance with the procedure laid down in this article.
